criteria.label: Explain the four file spaces (working tree, index, local repo, remote) and which command moves a file between each.
criteria.example: `vim file` → working tree → `git add` → index → `git commit` → local repo → `git push` → remote.

criteria.label: Stage selectively with `git add -p` to split unrelated changes into separate commits.
criteria.example: `git add -p src/auth.py` to commit only auth hunks; leave the README typo for a separate commit.

criteria.label: Cut and switch branches with `git switch -c <name>` / `git switch <name>` without thinking.
criteria.example: `git switch -c feature/rerank` then `git switch main` to context-switch.

criteria.label: Write imperative, body-bearing commit messages explaining the *why*, not just the *what*.
criteria.example: Subject: `Add retry on 429 from embedding API`. Body: explains the upstream behaviour and chosen backoff.

criteria.label: Recover 'lost' work using `git reflog` after an accidental delete / hard reset.
criteria.example: `git reflog` → spot the SHA before the bad action → `git switch -c rescue <sha>`.

criteria.label: Reason about when force-push is safe (private branch) vs catastrophic (shared branch).
criteria.example: Force-push to `feature/my-private`: OK. Force-push to `main`: never. Use `--force-with-lease`.
